events.label: reasoning
events.text: The scenario: aluminum conductors on a device not rated CO/ALR. This is a defect - aluminum wire connected to receptacles/switches not rated for aluminum (CO/ALR). This is a fire hazard due to galvanic corrosion and thermal expansion issues. Fail.

"decision": "fail",

"findings": [
"Aluminum conductors terminated on a device not rated CO/ALR — incompatible termination, fire hazard due to oxidation/thermal expansion"
],
"actions": [
"Replace device with one marked CO/ALR, or use approved pigtail/splice method with antioxidant compound and listed connectors",
"Verify all aluminum terminations on the circuit are CO/ALR-rated or properly spliced to copper"
],
"rationale": "Aluminum conductors connected to standard (non-CO/ALR) devices are a known fire hazard due to differential thermal expansion and oxidation causing loose, overheating connections. NEC requires listed/rated terminations; device must be marked CO/ALR for aluminum.",
"references": [
"NEC 110.14 (electrical connections; listed/rated terminations)",
"NEC 310",
"Table 310.16",
"NEC 404.14(C) / 406.11(C) (CO/ALR marking for devices with aluminum terminations)"
]
}
